IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). 2026-03-13T07:19:04.9637322Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T07:17:32.07877652Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57056,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Resource serialization instantiates abstract base type\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nThe generator emits `new AbstractType()` in resource serialization helpers for polymorphic/discriminated base types. Since these types are `abstract`, this causes CS0144 build errors.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n**6 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).\\n\\n## How to Reproduce\\n\\n1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`\\n2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net\\n3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs\\n4. Run code generation:\\n```bash\\ncd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src\\ndotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights\\n```\\n5. Build: `dotnet build`\\n6. Observe CS0144 errors: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface`\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe generator creates a static deserialization instance field pattern for resource serialization. For polymorphic base types (discriminated unions), it tries to instantiate the abstract base class directly instead of using the static deserializer.\\n\\n**Example:** `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` (line 19):\\n```csharp\\nprivate static IJsonModel DataDeserializationInstance \\n    =\\u0026gt; s_dataDeserializationInstance ??= new SecurityInsights.SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData();  // BUG: abstract type\\n```\\n\\nBut `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData` is declared as:\\n```csharp\\npublic abstract partial class SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData : ResourceData\\n```\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nFor abstract/polymorphic base types, the generator should use the base type\\u0026#39;s static `Deserialize` method or a non-abstract deserializer pattern instead of `new AbstractType()`.\\n\\n## Affected Files\\n\\n- `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData`\\n- `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionData`\\n- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryData`\\n- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateData`\\n- `SecurityInsightsEntityResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntity`\\n- `SecurityInsightsSettingsResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsSettingsData`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57056\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\",\"SecurityInsights\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-13T07:15:21Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-13T07:16:29Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-13T07:19:04.9735319Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T07:18:16.468141753Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":3,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}}
2026-03-13T07:19:04.9756744Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T07:18:43.453977405Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a well-scoped bug in the Azure Management Generator (`/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) where the resource serialization code emitter instantiates an abstract base type directly (`new AbstractType()`) for polymorphic/discriminated union models, causing **CS0144** build errors. The bug surfaces in the SecurityInsights migration (#56944) and affects 6 resource serialization files.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Classification\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Type** | Bug – code generator emits invalid C# |\n| **Severity** | High – causes build failure, blocks migration PR #56944 |\n| **Component** | `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` – resource serialization emitter |\n| **Service** | SecurityInsights (but fix lives in the generator, not the SDK) |\n| **Author** | @live1206 (MEMBER – part of mgmt generator CODEOWNERS) |\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe generator emits a lazy-initialized static field for `IJsonModel\u003cTData\u003e DataDeserializationInstance` in `*Resource.Serialization.cs` files. The initializer calls `new TData()`. When `TData` is an **abstract** class (due to discriminated-union / polymorphic TypeSpec models), the `new TData()` call is illegal C#.\n\n**Problematic pattern emitted:**\n```csharp\nprivate static IJsonModel\u003cSecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData\u003e DataDeserializationInstance\n    =\u003e s_dataDeserializationInstance ??= new SecurityInsights.SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData(); // CS0144\n```\n\nThe root of the problem is in the generator logic that chooses how to create the deserialization instance – it does not check whether the data model class is `abstract`.\n\n**Affected abstract types:**\n- `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData`\n- `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionData`\n- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryData`\n- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateData`\n- `SecurityInsightsEntity`\n- `SecurityInsightsSettingsData`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Debugging \u0026 Fix Strategy\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Locate the emitter code:**\n\nThe serialization code for ARM resources is generated in the mgmt generator. Start here:\n\n```\neng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/\n```\n\nLook for the provider or visitor that emits `*Resource.Serialization.cs` — likely a `ResourceSerializationProvider` or similar. Search for:\n- `DataDeserializationInstance`\n- `s_dataDeserializationInstance`\n- `IJsonModel`\n\n**Fix approach:**\n\nInstead of `new AbstractType()`, for abstract/polymorphic types the generator should use the **static `Deserialize` method** on the abstract base type (which already exists as part of the discriminated union pattern). Something like:\n\n```csharp\n// Option A: use a known concrete subtype's deserializer (not suitable if none exists)\n// Option B: use the static deserializer factory on the abstract type\nprivate static IJsonModel\u003cSecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData\u003e DataDeserializationInstance\n    =\u003e s_dataDeserializationInstance ??= SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData.DeserializeSecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData(/* sentinel element? */);\n```\n\nOr alternatively, bypass the lazy init pattern entirely for abstract types and call the static deserializer directly in the serialization method.\n\n**Key guard condition to add in the emitter:**\n```\nif (dataType.IsAbstract) { // use static Deserialize method instead of new() }\n```\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Find the emitter code in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` that generates `DataDeserializationInstance`\n- [ ] Add an `IsAbstract` check: when the data model is abstract, emit a call to the static `Deserialize` method rather than `new T()`\n- [ ] Write or update generator unit tests covering the abstract-base-type resource serialization scenario\n- [ ] Regenerate the SecurityInsights SDK to verify CS0144 errors are resolved\n- [ ] Verify no regressions in other mgmt SDKs by running `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- Parent migration issue: #56944\n- Mgmt generator source: [`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)\n- CODEOWNERS for mgmt generator: @JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang\n- [CS0144 error docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0144)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":57056},"name":"add_comment"}}}
2026-03-13T07:19:04.9775497Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T07:18:43.45977085Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":4,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}}
